title: MARTY SUPREME
logline: A young hustler from the Lower East Side steals from his uncle's shoe store to chase table tennis glory, only to lose everything—his money, his reputation, his relationships—before fighting for one final chance at redemption in Japan.
antagonistOrConflict: Marty's own ambition, impulsiveness, and self-destructive choices; external conflicts include his uncle Murray, Milton (rival/exploiter), sponge paddle controversy, and criminal consequences
tone: Gritty, darkly comedic, propulsive, emotionally raw,swith moments of absurdist chaos

actBreaks.description: Marty returns from Europe to face the consequences of stealing from his uncle—arrested, money taken, dreams derailed—ending his ascent and beginning his downward spiral.

validation.ruling: Scene #44 (p49, 29.2%) marks Marty's return to NYC after his humiliating tour culminating in playing against a sea lion. This irreversible plot twist fundamentally changes the story's direction from professional entertainer to desperate man seeking redemption, forcing him into new, higher-stakes conflict with his identity and self-worth.

actBreaks.description: After the violent confrontation at Hoff's farm and Rachel being rushed to the hospital, Marty hits absolute rock bottom, transitioning from chaotic survival into a final desperate gambit.

validation.ruling: Scene #114 (p116, 69%) shows the catastrophic failure of Marty's scheme—he's lost Dion's father's car and dog, destroyed relationships, and his lies are collapsing. This major turning point forces Marty into the final act where he must face the ultimate consequences and seek genuine redemption rather than continue his pattern of deception and shortcuts.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script follows a classic rise-fall-redemption arc, but subverts it with relentless momentum and moral ambiguity. Act One establishes Marty's hustle mentality and quick ascent through theft and talent. The structural pivot at scene 44 is brutal—returning home means facing immediate consequences, not triumph. Act Two becomes a sprawling descent: bathtub crashes, bowling alley cons, violent confrontations, romantic betrayals. The script doesn't pause for reflection; it accelerates through catastrophe. The second act break at scene 114 is visceral—Rachel in labor after violence—forcing Marty to finally choose something beyond himself. Act Three becomes a compressed redemption sprint: Japan, the rematch, the race home. The structure mirrors ping pong itself: fast volleys, sudden reversals, no time to think.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains breakneck velocity with an average scene length of 1.4 pages. Act One (scenes 1-44) covers roughly 49 pages—quick establishment of character, theft, European success, and immediate downfall. Act Two (scenes 45-114) is the longest at approximately 67 pages, deliberately sprawling through Marty's chaotic unraveling—the hotel bathtub disaster (scenes 60-70), New Jersey bowling con (scenes 72-82), and escalating relationship chaos (scenes 87-123) all compress time while extending desperation. Act Three (scenes 115-161) covers the final 52 pages with renewed urgency: confrontation, violence, hospital, Japan, and birth—matching the emotional acceleration. Key pacing shifts occur at the London tournament (scenes 19-27), the bathtub catastrophe (scene 67), and the final Japan sequence (scenes 148-161), where time compresses maximally.

incitingIncident.description: Marty lugs his suitcase up the stairs and encounters Judy leaving his apartment.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTERNOON

validation.ruling: Scene #4 (p5, 3%) is far too early and merely shows Marty being promoted to manager at a shoe store—a minor professional advancement that doesn't shatter his world or establish the main conflict. Scene #44 (p49, 29.2%) shows Marty returning to NYC after touring with the Globetrotters, having been humiliated by performing against a sea lion. This moment shatters his previous status as a professional entertainer and forces him back into his old life, establishing the central conflict of redemption and identity that drives the rest of the script.

midpoint.description: Milton confronts Marty for lying to his receptionist, but stops when Marty mentions wanting to revisit their conversation about Japan and offers to promote Milton's company in exchange for sponsorship.

validation.ruling: Scene #86 has no content available for evaluation. Scene #98 (p98, 58.3%) occurs after Marty's disastrous theatrical performance where he's exposed as a fraud in front of an audience. This public humiliation serves as a point of no return—he can no longer pretend to be someone he's not, forcing him to shift from reactive desperation to proactive confrontation with his choices and identity.

climax.description: Marty plays seriously against Endo and begins winning points, fighting back from 2-0 to tie the match as the G.I.s cheer while the Japanese crowd grows quiet.

validation.ruling: Scene #155 (p162, 96.4%) depicts the ultimate showdown between Marty and Endo in Japan before a packed amphitheater. This is the highest dramatic tension, where Marty confronts his ultimate obstacle (Endo), demonstrates his character arc completion by playing with integrity rather than showmanship, and resolves the main story question of whether he can find redemption through genuine mastery of his craft.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - STOCKROOM - LOWER EAST SIDE, NYC
scenes.summary: Marty Mauser enters the stockroom of Norkin Shoes.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty swaps shoes in a box, brings the wrong size to a customer, and tries to force them on her feet while claiming the brand runs small.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty heads to the back area of the shoe store.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - MURRAY’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty leaves a young woman waiting in the hallway while he enters Murray's office, and Murray tells him to come in.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty rejects Murray's promotion offer, insists he's not a shoe salesman, and demands to be paid before his trip, but Murray postpones settling up until closing.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - STOCKROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty and his girlfriend Rachel make out and have sex in the stockroom among the shoeboxes.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty practices intense table tennis against Wally at Lawrence's parlor while a phone rings and a misfit answers it.

scenes.heading: INT. PHONE BOOTH - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty enters the phone booth after arguing with Wally about his white shirt.

scenes.heading: INT. JUDY’S APARTMENT - LOWER EAST SIDE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Judy calls Marty claiming his mother is sick, but he's indifferent and suggests calling an ambulance instead.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - 2 FLOORS UP - MOMEN TS LATER
scenes.summary: Rachel overhears Judy and Mom staging the fake illness on the phone, then rushes into her apartment to warn Marty they're lying while evading her sick husband Ira.

scenes.heading: INT. HARDWARE STORE - MIDTOWN - SOON
scenes.summary: Rachel lies to Ira about who's lying and why she was delayed getting his broth.

scenes.heading: INT. GALANIS OFFICE - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty demonstrates to Galanis how an orange-painted ping pong ball is easier to follow than a white one, pitching it as a product investment that will revolutionize table tennis.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty pitches his orange ball innovation to a potential investor, establishing his entrepreneurial ambition beyond just playing.

scenes.heading: EXT. ORCHARD STREET - DUSK
scenes.summary: Galanis expresses limits on spending while Marty insists he'll regret not investing, then Marty runs across the street eating a hot dog.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - MAIN FLOOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lloyd tells Marty that Murray left to take his mother to the hospital, and Marty realizes he's been tricked out of getting paid before his trip.

scenes.heading: INT. NORKIN SHOES - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty pulls a gun from Murray's desk and threatens Lloyd to open the safe and give him the $700 he's owed, framing it as a legitimate robbery.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty commits armed robbery against his coworker to get his owed wages, a criminal act that will have consequences and shows his impulsiveness.

scenes.heading: EXT. 20,000 FEET IN THE AIR
scenes.summary: A Pan American propeller plane flies through the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPLANE - FIRST CLASS - DUSK
scenes.summary: Marty sips Coke in first class on the airplane.

scenes.heading: INT. LONDON CENTRAL HOSTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty arrives at a dingy London hostel where international players complain about the cold and rats steal food from their plates.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Players pose for a group photograph at Wembley Stadium where Marty notices three Japanese players and learns the travel ban has been lifted.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty watches Ted Bailey lose to unranked Japanese player Koto Endo, who uses a silent sponge paddle that defies physics and emits no sound.
scenes.turningPointNote: The introduction of the sponge paddle technology that will become central to the conflict and controversy in Marty's career.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty defeats his opponent Vladimír Sedlacek in Round 3.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - IATT OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty confronts IATT official Sethi about poor accommodations, complaining the hostel is a rat-infested dump compared to what star players deserve.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty holds court at a press junket at the Ritz Hotel, boasting he's the only star player while reporters get distracted by actress Kay Stone checking in with her husband Milton.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - ROYAL SUITE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Marty fabricates a sob story about being orphaned and abandoned to reporters who lose interest when they spot Kay Stone, then Marty watches Milton and Kay Rockwell cross the lobby.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - DOVER SUITE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Marty calls Kay's room pretending to be the front desk, tricks her into revealing she reads the Daily Mail, then shows her his newspaper photo and convinces her to meet him by throwing an apple through her window into a fruit bowl.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty defeats Béla Kletzki in an exhibition match where they perform trick shots, blowing the ball gently and forfeiting points while Kay watches.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - D INING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty dines with Béla and spots Kay across the restaurant, then sends champagne to her table and manipulates the situation to get invited to join Milton's group, where he charms everyone with stories and Milton reveals he's in the roller ball pen business.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU - 1944 - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Béla is forced to defuse bombs at Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - 1944 - SOON AFTER
scenes.summary: Béla works on bombs in the woods outside the camp, finishing quickly and pretending they're still active to waste time.

scenes.heading: INT. AUSCHWITZ-BIRKENAU - BUNKER - 1944 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Béla discovers bees in the woods while defusing bombs.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - TOP FLOOR - MIDDLE OF NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kay exits the elevator and approaches the Royal Suite in the middle of the night.

scenes.heading: INT. RITZ HOTEL - ROYAL SUITE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty answers the door in his underwear and Kay enters wearing only undergarments, then they have sex while Marty watches himself in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty competes against Koto Endo in the championship match.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty tells Kay in voiceover that Béla's Holocaust story is the most romantic thing he's ever heard.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty struggles against Endo's sponge paddle, first hitting hard to prevent returns, then switching to soft hits to reduce the sponge's effectiveness, while Milton watches stone-faced as the rallies become exhaustingly long.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty loses the championship to Endo's sponge paddle, a devastating defeat that shifts his trajectory and relationship to the game's technology.

scenes.heading: EXT. 5TH ARRONDISSEMENT - PARIS - DAY
scenes.summary: A narrator describes how Endo, who lost his hearing in the Tokyo Air Raid, tuned himself to the rhythm of table tennis and returns home humbly despite his victory.

scenes.heading: INT. LARUE - PARIS - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty approaches a Parisian restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. LARUE - DINING ROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty meets Milton for lunch in Paris, admires his custom watch, and Milton thanks him for introducing him to table tennis while ordering an incorrect salad to be replaced, then pitches Marty on mass-producing sponge paddles but Marty refuses, calling it cheating and insulting Milton by bringing up his murdered son.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty refuses Milton's business offer to mass-produce sponge paddles, creating a moral and business conflict with a powerful figure while also deeply insulting him.

scenes.heading: EXT. AUDITORIUM - BOSNIA - EVENING
scenes.summary: Marty and Béla perform with miniature tables on a basketball court in Athens.

scenes.heading: INT. AUDITORIUM - GREECE - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty and Béla entertain crowds with choreographed multi-ball rallies in Tangier.

scenes.heading: EXT. AMPHITHEATER - MOROCCO - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty poses with camels at the pyramids then chips a piece off a pyramid cornerstone with a pocketknife.

scenes.heading: EXT. PYRAMIDS - EGYPT - DAY
scenes.summary: The piece of pyramid stone breaks free in Cairo.

scenes.heading: INT. MID-SIZED ARENA - FRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty and Béla perform trick shots with five balls in France.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Marty lugs his suitcase up the stairs and encounters Judy leaving his apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Marty passes Judy without speaking and enters his apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters his sparse bedroom filled with trophies and clippings, sees his mother has left a glass in his room, and grabs it.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MARTY’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty returns the glass to the kitchen sink and coldly tells his mother not to eat or drink in his room.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty's mother awkwardly asks about his trip and offers him chicken while he washes the glass thoroughly and explains he was working and making money.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty's mother suggests he take a shower, and he returns with a ribboned box containing a jagged stone from an Egyptian pyramid as a gift for her.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - HALLWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty stands in the hallway after leaving his apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A uniformed cop waits in Marty's apartment and arrests him for armed robbery while Murray reveals he called the cop and took Marty's cash from his luggage to pay himself back.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty's uncle betrays him by orchestrating a fake arrest to extort money, forcing Marty to become a fugitive

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty confronts the cop about being paid off by Murray and offers to double the bribe for oral sex, prompting the cop to slap him across the face.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENEMENT BUILDING - FIRE ESCAPE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty escapes through his bedroom window onto the fire escape while Murray and the cop discuss lunch plans.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM
scenes.summary: Murray discovers Marty has fled through the window and the cop spots him climbing down the fire escape, alerting his partner below.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - LOWER EAST SIDE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty throws his duffle bag down to the sidewalk, makes eye contact with the second cop in the police car, and runs.

scenes.heading: EXT. ESSEX STREET - LOWER EAST SIDE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The second cop chases Marty on foot through the Lower East Side streets.

scenes.heading: INT. ESSEX STREET PETS - LOWER EAST SIDE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty enters Essex Street Pets and makes urgent phone calls.

scenes.heading: INT. ESSEX STREET PETS - BACK ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty calls Wally from the pet shop, lying to arrange a meetup while noticing pregnant employee Rachel, then rushes out a side door she opens when the cop enters through the front.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMMUNAL BACKYARDS/ALLEYWAYS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty vaults over concrete walls through communal backyards, jumps into garbage bags behind a Chinese restaurant, and hides under trash while the cop searches above.

scenes.heading: INT. HALSEY HOTEL - LOBBY - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty checks into the Halsey Hotel with Wally while a beat-up man pleads at a payphone and Mishkin arrives with a German Shepherd covered in feces.

scenes.heading: INT. HALSEY HOTEL - ROOM 503 - BATHROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty takes a bath in the hotel room.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally reclines in the main room reading Marty's mail while Marty shouts from the bathroom about his sexual prowess and continence technique.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty finishes bathing in the tub.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally discovers a bill showing the IATT has banned Marty and denied his World Championship application until he pays fifteen hundred dollars.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty continues his monologue about his urinary control technique from the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 403 - BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The bathtub crashes through the floor when Marty bolts upright upon hearing about the ban, landing on Mishkin in the bathroom below and pinning his arm while Marty applies a tourniquet to stop the bleeding.
scenes.turningPointNote: The tub collapse creates chaos that sends Marty fleeing the hotel and positions him with Mishkin's cash windfall

scenes.heading: INT. 5TH FLOOR HALLWAY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty rushes down the hallway wrapped in a towel while someone pounds on Mishkin's door.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 503 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty frantically packs his belongings while Wally confirms he's physically okay, and they prepare to leave the hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. 4TH FLOOR - HALLWAY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty and Wally exit the elevator into a flooded hallway where seedy guests loiter, and Marty enters Mishkin's room to confront the desk clerk.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 403 - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty argues with the desk clerk about the bathtub incident and demands compensation while workers try to fix the broken pipes, ultimately agreeing to transport Mishkin's injured dog to a vet in exchange for payment from Mishkin's cash-filled bag.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI CAB - LINCOLN TUNNEL - SOON
scenes.summary: Wally drives through the Lincoln Tunnel with the injured dog pacing in the backseat while Marty counts fifty dollars from Mishkin's cash-loaded satchel.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - CRANBURY, NEW JERSEY - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty insists to Wally they must hustle ping pong every night for two weeks to pay the IATT fine while feeding the dog bourbon and ice cream, causing it to choke.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - TABLE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty watches Wally play ping pong at the Bowlero and accepts a challenge from cocky Roger for a dollar per game.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLER O - TABLE 3 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally loses to Terry and declines his offer to keep the money, then tells Clark a fabricated story about being attacked by a cab fare.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - TABLE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Clark points out Roger as the best player in the room.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - LANE 5 - LATER
scenes.summary: Bowling pins explode nearby as Marty challenges Wally to a five-dollar game with Clark holding the money.

scenes.heading: INT. BOWLERO - TABLE 3 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Roger leaves his bowling lane to watch the growing crowd around Marty and Wally's ping pong match, then challenges Marty for forty dollars after Marty mocks him.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI CAB - CRANBURY, NJ - LATER
scenes.summary: Wally drives through New Jersey while Marty counts over two hundred dollars in winnings and sings along to Fats Domino with the dog Moses in the backseat.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNRAY GAS - SERVICE STATION
scenes.summary: Wally realizes the gas station ahead is closed but Marty tells him to pull in anyway.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNWAY GAS - SERVICE STATION
scenes.summary: Someone smashes through the gas station door and turns on the pumps.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNRAY GAS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Roger and his crew break into the darkened gas station and ambush Marty and Wally at the pump, dousing them with gasoline and smashing the car window before the cab crashes into a lamp post, flinging Moses and the cash onto the ground.
scenes.turningPointNote: The violent robbery strips Marty of his winnings and Moses, deepening his desperation and sending him back to Manhattan broke

scenes.heading: EXT. POTATO FIELD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wally refuses to go back for the dog and drives away furiously as Marty screams at him.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAWRENCE’S - 53RD AND BROADWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty arrives at Lawrence's ping pong parlor and receives shocked recognition from players who haven't seen him since his London loss.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE'S PING PONG PARLOR - STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty asks for Lawrence at the cage and heads toward the back of the parlor.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE'S PING PONG PARLOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty walks through the crowded parlor to Lawrence's office.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lawrence tells Marty that pregnant Rachel is waiting for him in the office, and Marty finds her with a black eye and suitcase, learning her husband Ira attacked her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel's arrival with injuries from domestic violence shifts Marty into protector mode and adds personal stakes beyond ping pong

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MARTY’S ROOM - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty grabs his championship trophy from his childhood bedroom in the Mauser apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUSER APARTMENT - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty leaves the apartment with his luggage and trophy.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT BUILDING - HALLWAY AND STAIRS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty pounds on Ira's apartment door and bashes him in the face with the trophy base when he answers, then leaves with Rachel on the subway.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY CAR - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel travel together on a sparsely populated subway train with their luggage.

scenes.heading: INT. DION’S APARTMENT - PETER COOPER VILLAGE - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty brings Rachel to Dion's apartment and falsely introduces her as his sister, where Dion awkwardly accommodates them.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dion shows Marty boxes of orange Marty Supreme ping pong balls and custom packaging he's invested in, while Rachel rests on the bed.

scenes.heading: INT. DION’S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Marty tells Rachel he needs to raise money to get to Japan while she rests in bed, then calls Milton Rockwell's office from Dion's living room and learns Milton is at an off-site theater rehearsal.

scenes.heading: EXT. THEATER DISTRICT - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty walks toward the Morosco Theater after the receptionist tells him Milton is at a rehearsal there.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty enters the Morosco Theater hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - BACKSTAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty sneaks backstage and watches Kay rehearse a violent scene where a son stabs a suitor, then helps unstick a jammed door and flirts with Kay before spotting Milton walking past and following him out.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty follows Milton down the hallway and overhears him berating the production manager.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOROSCO THEATER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Milton confronts Marty for lying to his receptionist, but stops when Marty mentions wanting to revisit their conversation about Japan and offers to promote Milton's company in exchange for sponsorship.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty successfully pitches Milton on a sponsorship deal that could fund his Japan trip, turning his desperation into opportunity

scenes.heading: INT. EDISON HOTEL - BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Marty has sex with Kay in the Edison Hotel shower and secretly steals her expensive necklace, letting it slide down the drain.

scenes.heading: INT. EDISON HOTEL - BEDROOM - SOON
scenes.summary: Kay gets dressed and tells Marty she'll have more freedom after her husband Milton leaves for Japan following the play's opening, while Marty probes for information about Milton's trip.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty discovers Kay is Milton's wife and realizes he's sleeping with his potential sponsor's wife while scheming to exploit the situation

scenes.heading: INT. EDISON HOTEL - BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Kay confronts Marty about his recklessness, and he snaps back that he's old enough to fuck her in a hotel room during her comeback before she storms out, slamming the door.

scenes.heading: INT. UNCLE SAM’S PAWN SHOP - MIDTOWN - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty calls about sending a cashier's check for his fine while a custodian recovers Kay's necklace from a clogged drain, and Marty excitedly claims his wife would kiss the custodian if she were there.

scenes.heading: EXT. CRANBURY NEW JERSEY - LATE AFTEROON
scenes.summary: Marty drives Rachel in the Galanis' car through rural New Jersey while on the phone with Dion, instructing Rachel to retrieve a dog collar from his jacket pocket.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel tells Dion she has morning sickness and asks him to get ginger while Marty pulls into a gas station where Rachel retrieves Moses' dog collar from his jacket.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CRANBURY - SOON
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Marty spots a clapboard farmhouse and decides to try it, driving down the gravel driveway and knocking on the door where a dog barks inside before following the sound to a window.

scenes.heading: INT. HOFF’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty breaks into Hoff's house trying to retrieve Moses but the dog won't come, and when Hoff returns with a shotgun threatening to shoot Marty's nose off, Rachel frantically honks the horn as Hoff fires into the dirt.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty refuses to leave without the dog despite Hoff threatening to shoot, and as Hoff loads another shell Rachel desperately screams for Marty to get in the car.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. KAISER - CONTINIOUS
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: EXT. USED CAR LOT - LATER
scenes.summary: Rachel crashes the car into Hoff's porch while Marty scrambles in as Hoff fires his shotgun, then they drive through a cornfield at night before calling Moses' owner from a phone booth claiming they found the dog at an abandoned gas station in New Jersey.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty and Rachel's reckless attempt to steal the dog escalates to violence and forces them to create an elaborate cover story

scenes.heading: INT. DION’S APARTMENT - PETER COOPER VILLAGE - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty and Rachel sneak back into Dion's apartment late at night and are confronted by Dion who angrily whispers that his parents are sleeping, while Marty complains he should be practicing instead of making prank calls at a used car lot.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dion's father Galanis angrily demands Marty leave while Marty tries to defend himself, claiming he's giving Dion an amazing business opportunity, before Rachel bursts into tears asking where they'll go as Marty accuses Dion of throwing a pregnant woman onto the street.

scenes.heading: EXT. PETER COOPER VILLAGE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. PETER COOPER VILLAGE - ELEVATOR - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty adopts a friendly demeanor claiming he's picking up Supreme samples for a Spalding meeting, but Galanis doesn't care what Dion says and insists Marty get out of his house, accusing him of taking advantage.

scenes.heading: INT. PETER COOPER VILLAGE - LOBBY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty tells Rachel he has a purpose while she doesn't, explaining his life is shaped by obligation to see something specific through while hers results from just making it up as she goes along.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - LOWER EAST SIDE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Marty tells Rachel he'll help her find a maternity home where the baby can be placed with a good family, but she quietly refuses before he tells her to go home to her husband and exits.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. RACHEL’S APARTMENT - DAWN
scenes.summary: Rachel tells Ira the baby isn't his while Marty practices table tennis with ferocious intensity, and Ira rages through the apartment destroying furniture and dumping Rachel's clothing before setting it on fire.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel's marriage explodes as she confesses the pregnancy isn't Ira's, creating total chaos in her life

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - DAWN
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEWARD PARK - SOON AFTER
scenes.summary: Ira throws Rachel's luggage into the hall declaring she's her mother's problem now while Rachel apologizes to neighbors, then Lawrence leaves Marty's parlor as Marty turns off the lights and stares at a promotional standee of himself before Rachel meets Mishkin and Mitch in Seward Park.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDGE OF PARK - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A bystander stops Mishkin thinking he's attacking Rachel, pinning Mitch to the ground while someone calls the police, as Rachel sees Mishkin nearly reaching Moses and turns to run the opposite direction.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - DRESSING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Mishkin grabs a bartender by the throat demanding answers and drops the dog's leash, causing Moses to run into traffic where Mishkin stabs the bartender with a knife, while Kay signs thank you notes in her dressing room before Marty arrives asking to take her to lunch.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - BOX OFFICE - SHOWTIME
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. MOROSCO THEATER - SOON
scenes.summary: Kay coldly refuses to have lunch with Marty, and when he nervously pulls out the necklace he stole from her, she bursts out laughing and tells Merle to get Marty a seat for opening night.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PARK - JUST PAST MIDNIGHT
scenes.summary: Marty receives an envelope with a ticket and a note from Kay asking him to meet her in Central Park at midnight, then watches Kay perform on stage with intensity while a chair is thrown and the audience applauds her entrance.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREAT LAWN - SOON
scenes.summary: Kay gives Marty her earrings to cover his trip expenses before pushing him against a tunnel wall to kiss him, then they fall onto the grass where she pushes his head between her legs until a patrolman's flashlight finds them.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIFTH AVENUE - SOON
scenes.summary: The patrolmen threaten to arrest Kay and Marty, but Kay offers them cash and her necklace as a bribe, then she and Marty rush away as she asks incredulously if he wants another necklace.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: EXT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - 2ND FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kay enters the party where Milton angrily confronts her for disappearing and making him look like an idiot, then tells her Merle is on the phone getting the New York Times review read to him.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - 3RD FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - 2RD FLOOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Marty navigates the thinning party with a muted vibe, avoiding Milton seated in a den, then climbs another flight where a high society woman refuses to let him see Kay, but he glimpses Kay crying hysterically inside the room.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kay's opening night ends in devastation, likely from a terrible review, destroying her comeback

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - STUDY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. 870 FIFTH AVENUE - DEN - MOMENTS LATER 168A 168A
scenes.summary: Marty begs Milton for help, getting on his knees in total supplication and promising to do anything, then Milton sends him to retrieve a promotional sponge paddle from his study before making Marty agree to an unspecified humiliating act.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY - AFTER
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. LAWRENCE’S PING PONG PARLOR - LATERN
scenes.summary: Milton tells Marty the paddle strike is for his son, then hits Marty's ass as hard as he possibly can before Marty rides the subway in dejected numbness and tells Lawrence he's crashing in the office before his morning flight to Japan.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty suffers ultimate humiliation from Milton but secures his trip to Japan, marking a devastating low point and transition

scenes.heading: INT. NASH AMBASSADOR - CRANBURY, NJ - LATER
scenes.summary: Mishkin calmly tells Marty he can say goodbye to his friend Wally, but when Marty asks where Mishkin will be, Mishkin says he'll be at the car taking a hammer to Marty's girl's stomach.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: INT. NASH AMBASSADOR - CRANBURY, NJ - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty directs Mishkin and Mitch to Hoff's farmhouse where they get out of the car leaving Rachel inside with Reuben, then approach the dark house as Marty knocks on the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOFF’S FARM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mishkin pounds on Hoff's door as Mitch peers through a window, then a rifle shot shatters the glass hitting Mitch in the face while Marty covers his head and Mishkin pulls a gun, firing back as the Ambassador's window shatters and Rachel screams.
scenes.turningPointNote: The confrontation at Hoff's farm erupts into deadly violence with Mitch shot in the face

scenes.heading: INT. NASH AMBASSADOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel screams at Marty to get the money from Mishkin's inside pocket, so Marty rifles through the dying man's coat and discovers the cash is mostly cut-out pornographic magazine pages before hearing Moses crying inside the house.

scenes.heading: INT. BELLEVUE HOSPITAL - ER - LATER
scenes.summary: A nurse rushes Rachel on a gurney asking when her due date is while Rachel panics about the baby, then they reach double doors where the nurse tells Marty he can't come further as Rachel screams his name and begins hyperventilating.

scenes.heading: INT. HANEDA AIRPORT - JAPAN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Scene heading only, no content provided.

scenes.heading: EXT. JAPANESE COUNTRYSIDE - SOON
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - LEFT OF STAGE
scenes.summary: No content provided for this scene.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - CONCESSION STANDS - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty waits in line disguised at Endo's challenge event where a humiliating mural of his London defeat hangs nearby, while Milton speaks with a U.S. General and press gather to document challengers losing points to Endo.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - BY THE STAGE - AFTER
scenes.summary: Sethi refuses to let Marty into the tournament despite his journey to Japan, bluntly telling him he's wasted his time because Sethi doesn't like him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty hits rock bottom when Sethi personally blocks his tournament entry, forcing him to take the public humiliation route through Milton's challenge.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty publicly challenges Endo as a fraud, and Milton arranges a match with the condition that the loser must kiss a pig in front of the audience.

scenes.heading: EXT. UENO PARK AMPHITHEATER - LATER
scenes.summary: Marty plays seriously against Endo and begins winning points, fighting back from 2-0 to tie the match as the G.I.s cheer while the Japanese crowd grows quiet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty stops throwing the match and demonstrates his true skill against Endo, beginning his redemption through authentic play rather than spectacle.

scenes.heading: EXT. YOKOTA AIR BASE - AIRFIELD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: No content provided for this scene.

scenes.heading: INT. DC-4 AIRPLANE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty calls the hospital looking for Rachel and learns she was moved to the maternity ward, then rushes through the military base in pouring rain to board a transport aircraft.

scenes.heading: INT. STEWART AIR NATIONAL GUARD BASE - HUDSON, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: No content provided for this scene.

scenes.heading: INT. BELLEVUE HOSPITAL - MATERNITY WARD - SOON
scenes.summary: No content provided for this scene.

scenes.heading: INT. MATERNITY WARD - ROOM 613 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Marty panics when told Rachel was rushed to maternity, races through the hospital past the nurse's station claiming to be the father, and sees his mother seated with family members in the waiting area.

scenes.heading: INT. MATERNITY WARD - NURSERY - SOON
scenes.summary: Marty finds his baby in the nursery marked 'Mizler,' and as the nurse wheels the bassinet close to the window, he breaks down sobbing uncontrollably.
scenes.turningPointNote: Marty becomes a father and experiences a cathartic emotional breakthrough, completing his character arc from self-destructive ambition to genuine human connection.
